bundles.changelog: First concrete foundation-bundle prerelease package (six foundation-document templates with their own schema and hash baselines).
Reworks the technical-architecture document so cross-cutting content lives in one place and is cross-referenced rather than duplicated.
Your built system can now APPLY foundation-document upgrades (not just detect them); upgrades became repeatable, quieter, and clearer, and merge your edits with the new version where possible.

bundles.recommendation_reason: You now build and run your system one capability at a time, accepting each before the next.
bundles.changelog: You now build and run your system one capability at a time, accepting each before building the next (the build-and-operate loop with per-phase acceptance).

bundles.recommendation_reason: Adds operator orientation and risk-action protections, and lets your system safely apply its own updates.
bundles.changelog: Your system now keeps you oriented and protects risky actions; can receive operating-layer improvements through an upgrade (not just foundation documents); and can check for and safely apply its own updates.

bundles.recommendation_reason: The approval drill now uses an example grounded in what your system actually does.
bundles.changelog: The approval drill (the one-time safety demonstration) now uses an example grounded in what your system actually does, instead of a generic action.

bundles.recommendation_reason: Makes updating your system one safe step and hardens two update-safety details.
bundles.changelog: Updating your system is now one safe step: the assistant refreshes the update tool and applies the update together. Also hardens two safety details — the record of an approved update can no longer be altered, and the start-of-session update check uses the same trusted source as the manual check.

bundles.recommendation_reason: Fixes a safety-check bug that was interrupting your ordinary edits and training you to click through every prompt.
bundles.changelog: Fixes a safety-check bug that was stopping you to approve ordinary edits to your own notes and documents — everyday words like "firm" or "High" were mistaken for risky commands, which both broke auto mode and trained you to click through every prompt. The check now decides by what an action actually does, so it pauses only for genuinely risky, irreversible, or outgoing actions. Also adds a short note explaining why auto mode still pauses before sending, sharing, or deleting.

bundles.recommendation_reason: Stops your system from talking you out of safe updates — the update check now reports a clear recommendation and never treats a normal prerelease label or missing notes as a reason to wait.
bundles.changelog: Fixes the update flow so your system no longer talks you out of safe updates. When you ask "what's new?", it now runs the update check and reports exactly what the check found — a plain-language summary, the system's own recommendation, and the one command to apply it — instead of a vague walkthrough. A version being labeled prerelease or missing detailed notes is never treated as a reason to wait, and if your update tool is behind, the check routes you straight to the one-step refresh-and-apply.

bundles.recommendation_reason: Makes updates safer to approve: your system now shows you a read-only preview of exactly what an update changes before anything is applied, and the apply installs exactly what you previewed.
bundles.changelog: Improves how updates are previewed and applied. When an update is available, your system now runs a read-only preview that shows exactly what would change -- and that preview works even when the update tool is behind, so it can never wrongly tell you there is 'nothing to show'. You then approve, and the apply installs exactly the version you previewed (if the official source moved in between, it stops and re-previews rather than installing something different). The apply command is also kept short so it can't break when pasted.

bundles.recommendation_reason: Makes updating smoother: your system keeps offering the remind-later / skip / not-now choices for an update, and once you approve it runs the update for you instead of asking you to type a command.
bundles.changelog: Smooths out the update experience. When an update is available your system keeps offering the full set of choices -- see what's new, remind me later, skip this version, or not now -- even if you've asked it to lead with a single next step; remind-later and skip are remembered so the reminder actually pauses. And once you approve an update, the assistant runs the update commands for you (resolving the tool's location so they can't fail with 'command not found') rather than asking you to paste them into a terminal.

bundles.recommendation_reason: A small consistency fix to the update process -- your system now follows one clear path to check for and apply updates.
bundles.changelog: Simplifies and makes consistent how updates are checked and applied. Your system now uses one clear path -- it checks (which reports what's new and its recommendation), and once you approve, the assistant applies the update for you. A separate preview step that the system's own instructions did not actually use has been removed, so the update flow behaves the same way every time.
